title: ICAI and CPA Australia Renew Mutual Recognition Agreement for Five Years
content:
# ICAI and CPA Australia Renew Mutual Recognition Agreement for Five Years

Ek important update hai for all Chartered Accountants! The Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) and CPA Australia have renewed their Mutual Recognition Agreement (MRA) for another five years. This renewal, announced on July 10, 2026, reaffirms their commitment to strengthening the global accountancy profession.

Here are the key details:

*   **What is it?** The MRA establishes a framework for recognizing the professional qualifications of eligible members from both ICAI and CPA Australia.
*   **Why is it important?** This agreement facilitates greater professional mobility for CAs between India and Australia, while ensuring the highest standards of competence, ethics, and professional practice are maintained.
*   **Duration:** The renewed agreement is for a further period of five years.
*   **Signing Ceremony:** The Extension Agreement was formally signed at the ICAI Headquarters in New Delhi. ICAI President CA. Prasanna Kumar D and CPA Australia CEO Mr. Chris Freeland AM were present during the signing.
*   **Benefits:** This renewal is expected to enhance collaboration in professional development, capacity building, and global mobility, creating more opportunities for members in both countries.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.icai.org/post/international-affairs-committee)

*Published on: 10 July 2026*
